sourceMovie.title: Twins
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Twins for the same reason it broke through in 1988: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ivan Reitman keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Despicable Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Twins clicked for you, Despicable Me (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Twins?
faq.answer: Try Despicable Me, The Boss Baby, Lilo & Stitch—each shares DNA with Twins while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Twins?
faq.answer: Twins (1988) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Twins worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
